segment-text: Kristen Wiig & Annie Mumolo Lionsgate Entertainment
All Rights Reserved MUSIC: "GUILTY" (Barbra Streisand and Barry Gibb)
EXT. PEACEFUL SUBURBAN NEIGHBORHOOD - BEAUTIFUL DAY

CLOSE UP: LITTLE BOY'S legs, red sneakers with white socks, pedaling fast on a bicycle. Basket full of newspapers. Reveal it's a CUTE ASIAN BOY wearing A YELLOW BASEBALL HAT and "typical" young boy clothes. He's wearing headphones, happy. As the song's lyrics begin, he LIP-SYNC's along perfectly as he tosses the newspapers into yards. The BOY KEEPS RIDING UNTIL...
EXT. SMALL BLUE HOUSE - CONTINUOUS
He STOPS riding, no longer smiling. He throws a NEWSPAPER onto the front porch. With a menacing look, bikes away.
EXT. RURAL STREETS
He rides through a more rural area. Fewer houses. Scenery is changing. Even less houses. Keeps riding.
EXT. FARMLAND

Now in total FARMLAND, he stops. He looks around suspiciously and takes his headphones off. MUSIC stops. A LONE TREE in the middle of a distant field. We hear the wind as the leaves blow. He walks his bike across the dry field to the tree. As the boy looks up at the branches, A FAKE MECHANICAL OWL turns his head to the boy, a red light appears in its eyes, scans the boy and suddenly a DOOR IN THE TREE TRUNK OPENS revealing a metal ELEVATOR. The boy ENTERS, pushes the only button, it reads "LAIR." Doors close.
INT. ELEVATOR - CONTINUOUS
As he rides, we see SHEAR ROCK FACE pass through the small window behind him. We are descending deep into the earth.
INT. UNDERGROUND HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
The elevator doors open, we follow the BOY'S FEET WALKING along shiny metal floors. Soon another set of feet join his. SECURITY GUARD (O.S.) Morning Yoyo.
Morning Gene. Has it started?
Tilt up:
They're waiting for you sir. You better get changed.

YOYO, the boy, stops at a METAL DOOR, scans his fingerprints, the door opens. He enters, the door shuts and he immediately comes back out wearing a SUIT and an EARRING, fully dressed like a man. He crosses the hall to large doors, scans, and walks through.
INT. LAIR - CONTINUOUS

YOYO'S POV: Camera slowly enters revealing a reddish lit living room, dark wood walls, brown leather COUCHES, a HUGE BEAR SKIN RUG, many CANDLES, and some unnaturally large ones. AN ELDERLY SCIENTIST in a WHITE COAT waits alone under a bright light. He looks over, leaning against a nearby wall is EDGAR PAGET, early 40's. Handsome, impeccable, serious. We hear a THROAT CLEARING, Edgar stands up straight. We see the SILHOUETTE of a TALL WOMAN emerge from the shadows of the room. As she comes into the light, her all-white and cream FLOWY CLOTHING almost glows. This is SHARON GORDON FISHERMAN. Her hair is black, a futuristic hair cut we've never seen before. Her skin is flawless and pale. She looks to Yoyo, "Did you do it?". He nods back. She smiles maliciously. She is beyond intimidating, icy. Scientist is nervous.
Would anyone like a drink before we get started?
No thank you.
Well I'm going to make myself a "suicide". Are you familiar with that drink, Dr. Bradley?

He shakes his head no. She walks gracefully, taking full advantage of her cape, and slowly approaches a 7-11 type SODA MACHINE. She takes a LARGE PLASTIC CUP and fills it with ice, which falls loudly into the cup. She then begins filling the cup with each soda. SHARON GORDON FISHERMAN (CONT'D)

A little bit of Root Beer, traditional cola, a touch of Lemon Lime Fizz, Orange Up and my secret ingredient... a dash of lemoninfused Iced tea. I like the extra bite. She sips from the cup, then goes back to the Root Beer for one last splash. She, Yoyo and Edgar approach the scientist.
Is it ready?
Finally... yes.
SHARON and Edgar share a pleased look. Wonderful.
SHARON GORDON FISHERMAN
They walk towards a room with a covered window. As the curtain opens...
After months of work, I have genetically modified the mosquitos.
They look into a stark-white room where a COW stands. There's a small black box on the floor near its feet. Yoyo evilly cracks his knuckles, SHARON gives him a "stop it" look.
This remote here, activates that receiver under the cow, releasing a
signal instantly attracting the
These aren't just itchy mosquito bites. One sting will kill a large animal within minutes. Show me.

The scientist flips a switch. Inside the room a small PANEL on the wall opens. In it, ONE MOSQUITO. The scientist presses the remote control. The receiver LIGHTS UP. The mosquito flies directly towards it, stings the cow. It lets out a painful moo, Edgar winces. We hear the cow fall on the floor. Dead.
It's perfect.
The scientist looks on, proud. She begins pacing.

So, hypothetically speaking, if the receiver was placed, let's say, in the middle of a small town... the mosquitoes would then fly towards it? Stinging and killing everyone in the town? Hypothetically.
The scientist takes a second to comprehend.
Well... it depends, I mean if you have thousands of mosquitos, I guess you could kill thousands of people... but we wouldn't want to do that. Right?
Sharon smiles at him mischievously. He smiles back, unsure.
I mean that's not, not what this is for. Right? We are using these to kill animals with rabies? Right? Off her look.
In South... Africa? Right? They have a problem of rabid herds of animals? You said? That's why we did this? Right you guys?... Right?
His face falls. This was NOT the plan. Two guards approach.
(panicked) Wait! You can't get away with this! My house is full of evidence! I have all our e-mails, your checksSHARON GORDON FISHERMAN Oh we've taken care of that.
She nods to Yoyo. Quick zoom in as he pinches his earring. It lights up. "Beep". The newspaper beeps, the house explodes. Debris flies.
INT. LAIR - CONTINUOUS

The two guards drag the scientist into the white room. As he screams for his life, Sharon presses the remote. There is an ominous buzz, as hundreds of mosquitoes are released into the room as he continues to scream. Edgar can't look. SHARON GORDON FISHERMAN Goodbye Dr. Bradley. Off Sharon's smile, weCUT TO: BLACK MUSIC: "MAN! I FEEL LIKE A WOMAN" Shania Twain CHYRON: SOFT ROCK, NEBRASKA.
INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY

Various close up shots of two MIDDLE AGED WOMEN's body parts slightly moving to the music. Hands lightly slapping a thigh, a foot in a practical pump tapping along, wavy MIDWESTERN HAIR (think Tootsie) swaying along, small shoulder moves etc. This is BARB and STAR, 40-ish, sitting on a couch drinking tea. They are middle America, small-town women. Straight out of a CHICO'S catalog.
Whenever I watch those movies where you're in the 1800's, I can't stop thinking, did everybody just, you know? Gosh, I don't wanna be rude--
Stink? I think of that too! Those stories, when the daughter of the
king sneaks into the barn with a farmhand, doesn't he just reek of
They didn't have deodorant! There were no toilets. They just went all over the place! They didn't brush their teeth!
Well, I think I've seen an old timey toothbrush and they're wood and they have brown horse hair.
I want one of those. I wish I was alive then, and was still alive now. They sip their tea.
Here's a question. What do the Pennsylvania Dutch do?
I'll tell ya, they make peanuts. What!?

Yes! They're famous for these sugary peanuts. They sell them and they're making a pretty penny. I don't really know more than what I've already said and some of what I said, I'm not even sure I actually know. Excuse me. HI!
CUSTOMER (O.S)
The women turn to see a COUPLE standing there. Behind the couple is a sign for the store: JENNIFER CONVERTIBLES. This living room is in a furniture store.
Do you work here?
Yeah/Yes we do.
Great uh, we're interested in this couch that you're sitting on.
Barb and Star look at each other, nervous.
Oh, uh, this couch? This very one?
FEMALE CUSTOMER
Oh honey, it's a perfect fit. Barb and Star look at each other more panicked. They stand.
Oh good! The only thing is, this is
the floor model. And it's the only one left.
I'm sorry. Yeah, it's soiled up pretty badly.
We don't mind. We'll get it cleaned. We've just have been looking for a while. Do we get a discount if it's a floor model?
(swallowing) Ooh. I just gurgled a little bit.
Will you excuse us for a minute?
Barb and Star step aside.
What do we do!? This is our couch!
We've told each other so many things on this couch. Remember when you told me you were afraid you were addicted to caramel? That was on this couch.
Caramel squares. I'm glad that's over. And it was on this couch when you told me my husband was having an affair with Dina Rankusio.
That was so hard for me.
Well, you're a good friend for telling me. Some friends wouldn't.
Well, I couldn't keep that in! I would never lie to you.
I would never lie to you!
Excuse me ladies?
They give each other a crazy knowing look, then go back over to the customers.
You know...the reason why we're acting a little funny about this couch is... we really love it.
It's kind of our couch. We sit on this couch every day. It's our talking couch, you know?
So you're telling us not to buy this couch because you want to sit on it? At work?
Yes./We're saving up to buy it actually.
Well, since you don't own it, and it's for sale... we're going to buy it.
Barb and Star look at each other desperate.
Okay, well here's the real story. This couch is packed full of maggots.
And flies. They hatched and they're in there flying around in the cushions with other fly eggs that haven't hatched yet. You'll be able to hear 'em if you don't have the T.V. on real loud.
I don't think that's true.
Two separate ladies have passed
And their spirits are in this couch cause this couch has moved by itself and I've heard moaning. A lady moaning in it at night.
(annoyed with them)
So the couch is haunted.
Yep. And it's covered in our gas.
Excuse me?!
You can't help it on this couch. The way the cushions are, it just
spreads everything open.
We just fart on it all day.
You don't want this couch.
Well, you two just lost yourselves a sale.
The couple walk away in disbelief.
That was so close./We should rip a hole in the side of it. Hi!
Barb and Star's BOSS appears out of nowhere.
In my office.
The girls look at each other worried.
INT. BOSS'S OFFICE -
MOMENTS LATER They sit across from their BOSS.
I've called you guys in because-wait, Star are you even supposed to be working today?
Well I always come in when Barb's working and she does the same. Don't worry, you're not paying double.
Oh, I thought you were the one working today.
No! Oh well, that's funny/ It's not the first time!

I'm well aware. Listen guys, this is hard for me to do -BARB/STAR We're sorry about the couch/ I swear we're saving up for it. WeBOSS It's not that. That doesn't matter. The girls smile at each other, relieved.
Because we're closing the store. What?!
I think he means for renovations. (gasp) Are we getting a juke box?! Tell me we're getting that jukebox!
No, please stop asking me about that. The store is closing, for good. In fact corporate has decided to close 12 other stores for
strategic reassessment. The girls just stare, confused.
Sooo... you mean we can't come in? /But that means... Where do we... what are gonna we do?
I'm sorry, I know you love it here. Good news is the company's giving you severance.
He slides two envelopes over to each of them. They look at the envelopes in horror.
Severance? We don't wanna be severanced!
Please this job is our lives! It's our purpose! Where are we going to host Thanksgiving?
Wait, you came in when the store was closed and hosted your
Don't you two have a life outside the store? They don't.
Let me tell you a story. Summer of eighth grade I broke my leg.
Did you slip in the supermarket?
I always slip on those floors. Every time.

No! It was summer and I was stuck inside all day. But... with everyone out of the house and free time for my mind to wander, I found out I liked trying on my sisters bathing suits and making up dance routines! I was thrown a curve-ball and it was a blessing in disguise. And I think this is going to be the same for you two.
Barb and Star take this in, panicked. Beat.
So, we not allowed to come in tomorrow/The doors will be locked?
EXT. TOWN - MOMENTS LATER
The ladies exit the furniture store and walk to the town square park, mascara running. Clearly they've been sobbing.
Oh God I'm really upset. I think I'm grieving. I'm really grieving.
I am too but we'll just, find another job! This small town is full of places that are looking to hire women in their forties.
That's the attitude!
We'll find something even better! I mean, we both have high school degrees.
Well, you do.
Well I don't, I thought you did.
Well I don't. Gosh, what can we do?
Well let's think. We can always... hmmm...
They stop and think together, staring off into space. Hmm....
BARB/STAR

While staring off, a few people start gathering around, trying to figure out what they're staring at. A LITTLE GIRL walks up. LITTLE GIRL What are you looking at? More people gather. A HAPPY BRAGGY GUY finally pops in front. I see it! LADIES! WOMAN'S VOICE (O.S.) The crowd disperses as A WOMAN approaches. 40's. Beautiful, tight clothes and body, tan. Sun-visor over big hair, LOTS of shell necklaces. MICKEY REVELET. She throws up her arms. HI!!!!
Hi Mickey!/Wow! You look great!
Barb and Star watch her with admiration.

I know. I'm so tan. Miguel and I just got back from vacation. My dermatologist is not gonna be happy. I soaked up some mega rays. Ah! Lyin on the beach, though. Seven days of fun in the sun with my hon, warmin ma' buns... I'm not kidding. I feel like I had a souldouche.
BARB/STAR
You know we normally go to the Keys but we wanted to try some place different and girls, I might just pack up and move there.
What/Where? Where did you go?

This tiny little oasis on the coast of Florida. Palm trees swaying in the wind like Shakira, the ocean, cocktails, Jimmy Buffet music piped in everywhere, fudge shops to beat the band, best week of my life. You girls gotta go.
Us?! We can't do that. don't know, could we?
We can't. We need to stay here.

You have to! Listen, it's not the part of Florida where people go to die, no white hair and wheelchairs on the beach. And it's not Daytona either, with the teenage girls with their smart mouths and their belly rings, with their tih-tata's hanging out. It's our people. Midlifers who still wanna strut around the pool and stop the show in a tube top and full jewelry.
It does sound like a dream.
Not a reality for us.
And not that I was looking, but there are gorgeous men everywhere packing' their speedo's if you know what I mean. I'm talkin' Tommy Bahama, head to toe.
(in a trance) Tomma Hommy homma/Tommy bamommy...
Well, I gotta run. My step son stole my car and drove it through a Sherman Williams. He's ok but he lost a couple of toes and ripped his nose off. I'll drop off a brochure later.
That's not necessary!/Don't bother!
What was the name of the place again?
Mickey turns around and yells...
Vista Del Mar!
(trance-like) View of the swordfish/View of the mar.
INT. LAIR - DAY
CLOSE ON A MAP OF FLORIDA, VISTA DEL MAR has a RED BULLS-EYE drawn on it. Sharon Gordon Fisherman crosses in front of the map, pacing nervously. Edgar watches her, concerned.
What's on your mind?
I'm so nervous something is going to go wrong. I even tried to distract myself today by training some lab mice. (sighs/dramatic) Sometimes I wish I could just grab
on to a bunch of balloons and fly away.
Stop talking like that! Everything is going to be fine. I promise.
You leave in a few days. I want you to get there early. Get the lay of the land.
The rest of the arrangements have been made. Everything you'll need is in here.
She slides him an envelope that reads "ENVELOPE".
Now, here's the microchip. The receiver cannot be activated without it. Be careful with it.
He places it in its case which is his belt buckle.
And the antidote. Remember you might get stung but this will make you immune to the poison.
She takes out a small vile. He takes it, and her hand.
I wish you could come with me.
She quickly lets go.
(forced smile) You know I can't. It wouldn't be appropriate for me to go above ground at this point.
I won't let you down my love.
(serious, evil) No. You won't. I've been working on this plan my entire life. And now, it's finally time.
I can't wait for this to be over and for you to be happy, and we can be an official couple.
He moves towards her, she backs away a little.
(unconvincingly) Uh. Yeah. Uh-huh?
He kisses her. She turns to leave.
You do want to be together, don't you? To be an official couple?
Of course Edgar. I just can't relax
until this is over, so-She turns back to him with an icy stare.
Don't screw it up. SFX. DRAMATIC Musical sting.
REVEAL a small group of LAB MICE are in a little band. They put their instruments down, they just played the music.
Strings you're still off. The rest can have cheese.
INT. BARB AND STAR'S LIVING ROOM - MOMENTS LATER

CLOSE UP of a WINE CHARM on the bottom of a glass. Sitting in a circle is DEBBIE, PINKY, DELORES and BEV. They are all cut from the same mid-western, small town cloth. J Jill, Talbots, Chico's. Lots of pastels and hair do's. Barb and Star are doing their best to hide their stress.
Before we begin I wanna thank Barb and Star for hosting tonight's talking club, and for making their famous, usual hot dog soup. Again.
There is some light applause. Pinky is very petite and almost shaking. She gives a weird smile. The ladies sip their soup.
Well, look at the time. Gail's not here, but it is 6:00, on the dot.
Debbie gets up and locks the door. IMMEDIATELY there's a knock.
You're late Gail, goodbye! (to group, sitting) Talking Club is now in session. Everyone put their topics into the jar.
The ladies write on slips of paper. Barb leans in to Star.
(whispering) We can't tell the ladies what happened today!
What?! You mean lie? We can't!
We just lost our jobs at the hottest place in town. What will they think of us?!
But if they find out!DEBBIE Shhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhh hhhhhhhh. Ok, tonight's topic is...
Debbie reaches into a jar and pulls out a slip of paper.
DEBBIE (CONT'D)
Barb and Star shifts their wide eyes. Star blinks and looks around panicked. An excited Delores raises her hand.

I was hoping to talk this week about horses. Specifically my horse, and the spectacular time we had this weekend down by the creek. We got up at 4-DEBBIE Delores, you know the rules. We only share about the topic that we pull out of the talking jar. Give me your soup.
Delores shamefully slides her soup over. Debbie takes the soup and pours it in her own bowl.
I wanted to talk about my job at the Christmas store. I know everyone thinks its not busy right now. But we are...all year round. I believe you.
I don't like my job, but I like it more than I like being with my family.

Well, I love working at the pharmacy. I get to play with pills all day and look at them. I count them, I bottle them! Sometimes I shake them along to the music that plays! And I love my uniform! Its all white so I get to set myself apart with my character socks!
She lifts up a pointed toe showing socks with coffee cups with wings on them. Everyone oooh's and ah's.

(searching) Well... Today there was a delivery/ samples!/ guys brought stuff in the store/samples of tables/ 2 guys! One was so tall/We signed for it/couches came/ samples of fabric/ (then, together) So, we got a promotion!
Everyone claps and congratulates them.
What?! We are so proud of you. Let's do a toastDEBBIE I should do it!
Congratulations you guys!
Go home Gail! To Barb and Star...I always knew you'd go far. Haha! To future discounts!!
Everyone congratulates them. Everyone "Cheers!" Delores makes a horse sound.
Sorry, that's how I cheers with my horse.
Beat. Awkward silence. Everyone smiling at them. Star panics.
Barb and I lost our jobs! What?! Star!
It's true. The store's closing. The story we just told about the samples, and the guys and the tables, and the delivery was a lie! That's impossible!
She's right. We're sorry. We shouldn't have lied. Debbie stands. Get. Out.
What? Debbie no!/ We're sorry! Nobody stands up for them. They sip their soup and look down.
We all know, there are three major rules in Talking Club! Everyone scared, nods.
Number one- No sneakers. Number twoNo swearing except the "f" word, and number three- No lying.
Please no! Isn't there a three strike rule or something?
We won't do it again! Please! This is all we have left!
That's not surprising. What?
BARB/STAR

I might as well just say what we've all been saying behind your back, mostly me. You guys are kinda boring. It's always the same old things with you, nothing ever changes! You're two sad, single, middle aged women who live with each other. Nothing ever happens! You never really add anything exciting to the conversation!
Well that's only because they don't do anything exciting.
That's true.

I hope I'm not being mean, I just don't think any one of us want to be friends with you anymore. And before when I said "get out" I meant get out of the club. I knew you lived here and we're gonna get out now... by choice.
The ladies grab their purses and Delores grabs a saddle as they exit.
INT. BARB AND STAR'S BEDROOM - NIGHT
Two twin beds. Barb in one. Star enters in her nightgown.
Barb are you upset with me? I'm sorry I blurted it out.
It's ok, we lied. We deserved it.
Star climbs into bed. After a beat.
Barb, what if Jennifer Convertibles closing was a sign? And getting kicked out of Talking Club.
A sigh? What are you saying Star?
Ok, you know those days when you see me just staring at the carpet? Yes.
Sometimes I daydream about life outside of here. Don't you? Maybe something's telling us to--do something different.
To be honest... I have always wanted to try those shoes with the individual toes. What if we drank caffeine-No. This.
Star reveals the VISTA DEL MAR brochure Mickey dropped off.
Star. A trip? Right now?
We did get severance. I know we didn't know what that meant, but it's a check. Its not much but it's enough.
Star crosses to Barb's bed and shows her the brochure.
Just picture you and me on this Banana Boat. The two of us hitting a wave and getting launched into the air with our arms and legs spread open! The wind going everywhere. Inside us.

A banana boat? That's dangerous. Plus, a trip? Things happen to people on trips. What if we get lost, or poisoned, or get a rash. What if we get put in jail because they think we put drugs in our butts. What if we fall out of a car? The doctors don't have our charts, they don't know or medical histories. Have you heard of travelers diarrhea?
But Barb! I mean, haven't you ever wondered if the real ocean sounds like our noise machine?
The real ocean has strong currents. People get swept away, and they
scream... and then they sink. And then they die. Star walks over and sits on Barb's bed.

We used to have fun. I know things changed for you after Ron died, I mean they sure did after Carmine left me. But we're still those people! We used to sing duets at church. People loved it! We used to go on haunted hay rides.
We saw a real ghost.
Yes! We the Talent show at the Y when we roller-skated to the sound track of Free Willy. We used to write letters to Sally Field. And just tell her about our days. We just kind of... stopped.
I know you're afraid something's gonna go wrong, but what if its doesn't? What if we have the time of our lives? If we don't do something now we're dead anyway.
They look at each other.
It's like we use to have a, a-Shimmer.
Barb, I'm afraid we're fading away.
I don't want to fade away.
Barb takes a beat. Then a deep breath.
Let's do it. What?
Let's throw caution to the wind like a couple of rock and rollers! Really?! Yes!
AHHH! Let's go get a soul-douche.
They squeal with joy and hug each other.
INT. BARB AND STAR'S BEDROOM - DAY

CLOSE UP: Open suitcase thrown on bed. Each item thrown in: Traveler's checks, rape whistles, astronaut food, extension cords, passports, a Dura-flame log, ponds cold cream, calendar, curlers, Chicos catalogs, a crystal skull, etc.
AHHH!!! I can't believe we're doing this. We finally get to wear all those culottes we bought at Kaboom!
I packed tons of elastic waisted shorts because I'm gonna have a mouth full of clams casino morning to night.
INT. NEBRASKA AIRPORT - DAY
Barb and Star, in full vacation garb, ride the moving walkway, taking in the airport.
Wow. I haven't been in an airport in a while. I can't remember the last time. I think it's cause I never have.
About 12 years ago I came here for dinner. Gosh, it's changed.
Does this take us right onto the plane?
I assume so.
INT. AIRPLANE - LATER
Barb and Star in their seats, disheveled. They eat Ritz-bitz crackers out of a baggy.
Well I was not prepared for that takeoff.
Me neither. (to another passenger) Ma'am, I apologize for screaming at the top of my lungs.
Barb pulls a MAGAZINE out of the seat-back pocket.
What? A free magazine! I gotta read
this interview with Don Chee-adle.
You know that reminds me, the other day I said to myself... I can't think of a famous actress named Trish.
You're kidding. That's my favorite name.
Me too! How did we never talk about this?
To me, a woman named Trish is a woman you can count on.
Really has her act together.
Athletic, natural.
Just real natural. And loves the holidays!
Trish? At Christmas? Forget it. She
gets everybody a gift. Has a whole Santa's village in her front yard.
What about Halloween!? Oh Trish would leave a big pot of candy out for the kids.
Because she trusts!
She wouldn't be home anyway... she'd be out, with her girlfriendsSTAR Country-dancing. But still thinking of the kids.
Well she has a natural sense of rhythm. Big Heart... Trish.
INT. AIRPLANE - LATER
She'd have one ear double pierced. And the other-BARB Just single. Once when I was applying for a job at Talbots, I told them my name was Trish.
They laugh.
Well remember when I got in that hit and run and that lady passed? Her name was Trish!
Really?! I've just always wanted to answer the phone and say "This is Trish."
INT. AIRPLANE - LATER
Empty lunch plates on their tray. People are sleeping.
Trish loses one of her ears in a twister... but not her hearing.
She's a storm chaser.
Her mom does not want her to be.
But their relationship is tough. Trish always wanted to be a portrait photographer. She loves people. She would always say, "A person's face really says a lot about how they look."
That's beautiful.
INT. AIRPLANE - LATER
Some window shades up, flight attendants clear trash etc.
Trish's favorite number? 4-BARB 4! Her favorite animal?
Hen! Trish's favorite movie? Short Circuit./The Ring.
They just laugh and say "TRISH!"
INT. FLORIDA AIRPORT - LATER
Barb and Star look somber on the moving walkway, like something terrible has happened. Practically in tears.
And then she said "You know what, skin cancer? You're not gonna take
me. I'm gonna take my own life."
And she did. She jumped off that
cliff near her house on the Cape.
Now there's a beautiful spirit out there in the ocean and you know what her name is...
(emotional) Trish.
They grasp hands, almost in tears.
EXT. AIRPORT TERMINAL - CONTINUOUS
Edgar, looking very serious in sunglasses and a trench coat, is on his cell phone. I'm here.
He exits. RACK FOCUS to the girls exiting with their bags.
Wow. The air feels different.
Smells like Red Lobster. Oh look,
the shuttle for our hotel!

A fancy PINK SHUTTLE VAN pulls up. It has flamingos and an alligator with a cigar, tipping his hat. It reads "PALM VISTA HOTEL - Where Luxury Meets Coconuts." The shuttle doors open and Jimmy Buffet's CHEESEBURGER IN PARADISE comes blaring out the doors. Barb and Star excitedly board.
INT/EXT. PALM VISTA HOTEL - MAIN ENTRANCE - DAY

A full shuttle empties with happy tourists. Barb and Star exit the shuttle and stare in awe at THE LUXURY HOTEL-BARB/STAR Oh... my.. Stars./Oh... my... Barb. As the ladies enter lobby-MUSIC UP: In the style of "I Think I'm Gonna Like It Here" from Annie. A bellhop appears.
Let me take your bags ma'am.
Check in's over there.
I hope you had a nice flight.
FEMALE HOTEL STAFF
A HUGE MUSICAL NUMBER BEGINS! ("Welcome to Vista Del Mar")

- LOBBY, hotel staff including CONCIERGE welcome Barb and Star. Waiters walk with trays of tropical drinks offer the to ladies.
